slices.lastFinding: Gitar PR #62: chain-state bootstrap committed with `slices[0].prNumber: null` (the file was authored before the PR existed). Set to 62 in this fold-in so a resuming session can poll the correct PR via convention (d). fixRounds incremented to 1 — the (b) circuit-breaker arms at 3.

notes.schemaBootstrap: First write of .claude-code/auto-continue-state.json in this repo. Codifies the schema introduced by SESSION_START.md §193-230 convention (e), paired with the rewritten convention (d) that replaces `gh pr checks --watch` with a three-layer state-poll loop (CI + Gitar review + GitHub mergeable). Schema mirrors Throughline's docs/_meta/throughline/AUTO_CONTINUE_WORKFLOW.md §Chain State Persistence and the on-disk example at throughline's .claude-code/auto-continue-state.json.
notes.nextChain: FE-7 per FE_ROADMAP.md is the next phase chain; opening FE-7 will overwrite this file with the FE-7 chain's initial state (chainId=`fe-7-<slug>`, slices[] populated from the FE-7 plan, currentIndex=0).
notes.haltReasonVocabulary: haltReason is one of `spec-drift`, `circuit-breaker`, `user-pause`, or null (no halt). Matches the three named halt classes in §193-230 (a), (b), (c) respectively.
notes.writeSemantics: Writes are atomic (write-temp + rename). The file is committed so chain state survives session restart and is visible in `git status` / PR diffs. Per-slice `fixRounds` is the mechanical arming for the (b) circuit breaker — a fourth increment is the halt signal, not a fourth fix-round commit.
